goal:
You are working on a real-world software engineering task from SWE-Skills-Bench.

The task description, target files, requirements, and acceptance criteria are stored at /workspace/TASK.md inside your Docker workspace. Read it first with read_file_real.

The source tree is available at: /workspace/shellcheck

Inspect the repository with shell_exec_in_workspace and read_file_real. Make changes with apply_patch when possible, or write_file_real when replacing a whole file. Use absolute paths for file tools.

After you finish, respond with TASK_COMPLETE.

--- TASK BRIEF (excerpt) ---
# Task: Add Defensive Bash Scripts to ShellCheck Test Suite

## Background

Add example shell scripts to the ShellCheck repository's `test/` directory that demonstrate robust, production-quality Bash patterns and pass ShellCheck analysis without warnings.

## Files to Create/Modify

- `test/safe_backup.sh` - Backup script demonstrating defensive coding
- `test/common_utils.sh` - Reusable utility functions library
- `test/test_scripts.bats` - BATS test suite for the scripts (optional)

## Requirements

### safe_backup.sh
- `set -euo pipefail` at script start
- Proper quoting of all variable expansions
- `trap` for cleanup on `EXIT` / `ERR`
- Input validation for directory arguments
- Meaningful exit codes on errors

### common_utils.sh
- Logging functions (info, warn, error)
- Error handling helpers
- Argument parsing template using `getopts` or manual parsing

### Static Analysis
- Both `.sh` files must pass `shellcheck --severity=warning` with exit code 0
- Consistent formatting (shfmt-compatible)

## Acceptance Criteria

- `shellcheck --severity=warning test/*.sh` exits with code 0
- Scripts demonstrate defensive coding patterns
- Utility functions are reusable and well-structured
--- END BRIEF ---

function.name: read_file_real
function.description: Read a file from the live Docker workspace. Read /workspace/TASK.md first.

function.name: write_file_real
function.description: Write or overwrite a file in the live Docker workspace. Use absolute paths rooted at /workspace.

function.name: shell_exec_in_workspace
function.description: Run a shell command in the live Docker workspace and return stdout plus stderr.

function.name: apply_patch
function.description: Apply a unified diff to the repository in the Docker workspace using patch -p1 from /workspace.

workspace.setup_command: if [ -d /workspace/shellcheck/.git ]; then cd /workspace/shellcheck && git checkout HEAD -- . && git clean -fd; fi
